title: Object 33

[[File:Fireplace.gif|center]]
Object 33: "Memory Flame"
Description
{{CSS|content= .theme-fandomdesktop-light { --theme-page-text-color: #eeeeee; } }} {{Underline|FFFFFF}} {{Blurimage|file=Memorestoration3.png|caption=A bottle of Memory Flame.}} '''Memory Flame,''' also referred to as "Burning Memories," is a highly radioactive black colored element with the molecular symbol '''Me.''' It is well recognized for being extremely dangerous and erasing the user's memory, but it can also, very rarely, result in the user's death. Being near the Memory Flame is not advised due to its extreme risk, as even the smell of it can seriously harm one's lungs and in some cases result in death. As such, it should be approached only when it is absolutely necessary. While touching the Memory Flame doesn't usually cause memory loss, it leads to death. While it is usually found in nature as a liquid, it can occasionally be found as a solid or even as a gas. Memory Flame can pollute the air in a place and seriously harm people's lungs if it stays in an area for a long amount of time. Researchers are still studying Memory Flame and attempting to duplicate '''Memorestoration''', which opposes its effects. Keep in mind that these effects are irreversible and cannot be undone in any way aside of using '''Memorestoration'''. It is rare to find Memorestoration in nature, therefore if someone is affected by Memory Flame, there should always be someone who can find Memorestoration for them. Memory Flame should never be used on entities, as it makes them more aggressive rather than incapacitating them. Strangely, however, [[Entity 8|Hounds]] have been seen to run away from Memory Flame.
Effects
{{Underline|FFFFFF}} The user will experience some changes as a result of use over time. {| class="article-table" |+ !Time !Effects |- !5 Minutes |The user will experience a slight nausea. |- !10 Minutes |Nausea will increase and be replaced by a terrible headache. |- !20 Minutes |After twenty minutes, the user will slowly begin to forget what they experienced seconds ago. |- !30 Minutes |By the thirtieth minute, the physical effects will appear, and the user's skin color will begin to fade. |- !50 Minutes |The user will completely forget everything they have done in the last hour. |- !2 Hours |The user will completely forget every memory they have experienced throughout their life. At this stage, it has been reported that users are strangely affected by a feeling of sadness. |}
Origin
{{Underline|FFFFFF}} [[Jerry M. Sercher]] and a few [[B.W.O.]] members first discovered Memory Flame at [[Level 2]] in 1992 when they noticed it leaking from a pipe. There is no information available about Memory Flame's origins.
Locations
{{Underline|FFFFFF}} Although Memory Flame can be found in many levels of the Backrooms, it is most commonly found in [[Level 2]]. This liquid appears to flow through the pipes of [[Level 2]].
Compounds-Variants
{{Underline|FFFFFF}} '''MeO''': Red Flame
Mixing Memory Flame and '''Oxygen''' will result in a variant of Memory Flame, which is '''Red Flame'''. Its main difference from the Memory Flame is that '''Red Flame''' instantly kills the user instead of erasing their memory. Another difference is that it's color is red instead of black.
'''MeCt2''': '''Memorestoration'''
Mixing Memory Flame and [[Object 32|Corruptium]] will produce '''Memorestoration'''. {{Blurimage|file=Whitebackground.png|align=center|hovertext=Gone.|caption=Your memories.. They're.. gone.}} {{User:Muqon/Credits|image=Memorestoration3.png|author=Muqon|other=|audio=BurningMemory.mp3|audiovolume=0.5}} {{CSS|content= .header { text-align: center; font-size: 3em; transition: font-size 0.5s ease; } .header:hover { font-size: 3.2em; } .header2 { text-align: center; font-size: 1.8em; font-family: Glitch; transition: font-size 0.5s ease; } .header2:hover { font-size: 2em; } @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Merriweather+Sans:ital,wght@0,300;0,400;0,500;0,600;0,700;0,800;1,300;1,400;1,500;1,600;1,700;1,800&family=Merriweather:ital,wght@0,300;0,400;0,700;0,900;1,300;1,400;1,700;1,900&display=swap'); html .resizable-container { font-family: 'Merriweather Sans', sans-serif; } .page-header__title, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family: 'Merriweather', serif; }}
